message.content: Adversaries may exploit software vulnerabilities to collect credentials, targeting programming errors in programs, services, or operating systems to execute malicious code. This technique can compromise credentialing and authentication mechanisms, allowing attackers to gain access to valuable credentials or bypass authentication processes. A notable example is the exploitation of MS14-068, which targets Kerberos to forge tickets using domain user permissions. Successful exploitation can lead to credential access and potentially privilege escalation, depending on the targeted process or obtained credentials. Common adversary methods include identifying and exploiting unpatched vulnerabilities in software and leveraging these weaknesses to infiltrate systems. Mitigation strategies include regular patch management, employing robust vulnerability management programs, and implementing multi-factor authentication to reduce the risk of credential compromise.
